{
  "analysis": "The training data reveals a consistent pattern for German legal documents: Person entities appear in two main contexts. 1) As the judge/ruler (e.g., 'Richter Mag. Markus Knechtl', 'Richterin Dr.in Ursula Knechtges') following titles like 'Richter', 'Richterin', 'Mag.', 'Dr.', 'Prof.', etc. 2) As the party in the case (e.g., 'Beschwerdesache Hartmut Förg', 'Beschwerde der Bettina Müllenbrook') following phrases like 'Beschwerdesache', 'Beschwerde der', 'Beschwerde des'. The entities often include academic or professional titles (Mag., Dr., Prof., LL.M., Bakk. iur.) attached to the name. The challenge is to capture the full name including these titles without capturing the surrounding legal context (like 'Richter' itself if it's not part of the name, or the address). The pattern must handle titles with dots, spaces, and suffixes like 'in' for female forms.",
  "strategy": "I will create two primary regex rules. Rule 1 targets the Judge/Ruler context: matching titles (Richter, Richterin) followed by optional academic titles (Mag., Dr., Prof., etc.) and then the full name. Rule 2 targets the Party context: matching phrases like 'Beschwerdesache', 'Beschwerde der/des' followed by the full name. Both rules will use capture groups to isolate the name part, ensuring we don't include the preceding legal role words in the entity text unless they are part of the name's title (which they often are in this dataset, e.g., 'Mag. Gabriel Steinstrasser'). I will prioritize the specific title patterns to avoid false positives on common words.",
  "rules": [
    {
      "name": "Judge_Ruler_Person",
      "description": "Extracts person names of judges/rulers, often preceded by titles like Richter, Richterin, Mag., Dr., Prof.",
      "format": "regex",
      "pattern": "(?:Richter(?:in)?|Mag\\.|Dr\\.|Prof\\.|Hon\\.-Prof\\.|Priv\\.-Doz\\.|Univ\\.-Prof\\.|Bakk\\. iur\\.|Dipl\\.-Ing\\.|ÖkR|LL\\.M\\.|LL\\.B\\.)\\s+([A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:\\s+LL\\.M\\.)?(?:\\s+LL\\.B\\.)?)",
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "priority": 9,
      "reasoning": "Matches judges and officials. The pattern captures the title and then the name. The capture group $1 isolates the name part. Note: In some cases like 'Mag. Markus Knechtl LL.M.', the title is part of the entity text in training, so we might need to adjust. Looking at training: 'Mag. Markus Knechtl LL.M.' is NOT extracted as a whole, only 'Hartmut Förg' is extracted in the first example where 'Mag. Markus Knechtl' is the judge. Wait, re-reading Example 1: Input has 'Richter Mag. Markus Knechtl LL.M.' but Output only has 'Hartmut Förg'. This means the Judge's name is NOT extracted in Example 1? Let me re-check Example 2: Input has 'Richterin Dr.in Ursula Knechtges' and Output has 'Dr.in Ursula Knechtges'. So Judges ARE extracted if they have a title like Dr.in. But in Example 1, 'Mag. Markus Knechtl' is NOT extracted? Ah, looking closely at Example 1 output: Only 'Hartmut Förg' is listed. But Example 2 lists 'Dr.in Ursula Knechtges'. Why? Maybe because 'Mag.' is not considered a person entity in this specific dataset's annotation for judges? Or maybe I missed it? Let's look at Example 7: 'Mag. Gabriel Steinstrasser' IS extracted. So 'Mag.' IS included. Why not in Ex 1? Ex 1 text: 'Richter Mag. Markus Knechtl LL.M.'. Output: Only Hartmut Förg. This is inconsistent or I am misinterpreting. Let's look at Ex 1 again. Ah, maybe the rule is to extract ONLY the party (Beschwerdesache) and NOT the judge unless explicitly named as a party? No, Ex 2 extracts the judge. Ex 7 extracts the judge. Ex 10 extracts 'Hon.-Prof.in Lee Ortpaul' (judge) AND 'Fatima Herschelmann' (party). Ex 13 extracts 'Fabienne Jostl' (party) but NOT the judge? Wait, Ex 13 text starts with 'Ulrike Nussbaumer LL.M. M.B.L.' and output has only 'Fabienne Jostl'. So sometimes judges are extracted, sometimes not? This is tricky. Let's look at the pattern of extraction. It seems if the judge is mentioned with a title like Dr., Prof., Mag., they are extracted. But in Ex 1, 'Mag.' is present but not extracted? Maybe because it's 'Richter Mag.' and not just 'Mag.'? Or maybe it's a data error in my reading? Let's assume the rule should extract ANY person with a title or in a party context. I will refine to capture names after titles OR after party phrases."
    },
    {
      "name": "Party_Person",
      "description": "Extracts person names of parties involved in the case, following phrases like Beschwerdesache, Beschwerde der/des",
      "format": "regex",
      "pattern": "(?:Beschwerdesache|Beschwerde der|Beschwerde des|Antrag von|Vertreten durch|vertreten durch)\\s+([A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:\\s+(?:LL\\.M\\.|LL\\.B\\.|Bakk\\. iur\\.|Dipl\\.-Ing\\.|ÖkR|StB|RA|Steuermann|Wirtschaftsprüfer|KommR|Univ.-Prof\\.in|Hon.-Prof\\.in|Priv.-Doz\\.in|Mag\\.a|Dr\\.in|Dr\\.))?)",
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "priority": 8,
      "reasoning": "Captures parties in legal proceedings. The pattern looks for specific legal phrases and captures the following name and optional titles."
    },
    {
      "name": "Title_Person_General",
      "description": "Captures any person name that starts with a known title (Dr., Mag., Prof., etc.) even without specific context, to catch edge cases.",
      "format": "regex",
      "pattern": "(?:Dr\\.|Mag\\.|Prof\\.|Hon\\.-Prof\\.|Priv\\.-Doz\\.|Univ\\.-Prof\\.|Bakk\\. iur\\.|Dipl\\.-Ing\\.|ÖkR|LL\\.M\\.|LL\\.B\\.|StB|RA|KommR)\\s+([A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)*)",
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$0",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "priority": 5,
      "reasoning": "General fallback for any title followed by a name. Uses $0 to include the title as part of the entity text, as seen in many training examples (e.g., 'Dr.in Ursula Knechtges'). This handles cases where context is missing."
    }
  ],
  "deleted_rules": []
}

{
  "analysis": "The current rules suffer from severe over-matching (False Positives) and under-matching (False Negatives). \n1. **Party_Person**: The pattern `([A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)*)` is too greedy and lacks a stopping condition. It captures 'Beschwerdesache Rudolf Hummelt' (including the context word) and 'Bescheide' (a common noun). It also fails to capture suffixes like 'MA Bakk. art.' or 'RgR PhD'.\n2. **Title_Person_General**: The pattern `([A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)*)` after a title is too loose. It captures 'Lothar ' (trailing space) or 'Irvin Math' (cutting off at the umlaut or space if not handled correctly). It fails to capture complex titles like 'Mag.a Priv.-Doz.in Hon.-Prof.in'.\n3. **Name_After_Context**: Similar issues with greedy matching and lack of suffix handling.\n\nThe core issue is that the name capture groups are too generic (`[A-Z][a-z]+`) and do not account for:\n- Suffixes (e.g., 'MA Bakk. art.', 'RgR PhD', 'StB').\n- Hyphenated titles (e.g., 'Priv.-Doz.in').\n- The need to stop before addresses (commas, numbers) or other non-name words.\n- The inclusion of the title itself in the entity text when the title is part of the name (e.g., 'Hon.-Prof. Lothar Döppkens').\n\nStrategy: \n1. Replace `Party_Person` with a rule that strictly captures the name *after* context phrases, ensuring it stops at commas or addresses, and includes known suffixes.\n2. Replace `Title_Person_General` with a rule that captures the full title + name sequence, handling complex multi-part titles and ensuring the name is complete.\n3. Replace `Name_After_Context` with a stricter version that handles names without titles but stops correctly.\n4. Add a specific rule for names starting with 'RgR' or 'Ing.' which are common in this domain but currently fail.\n5. Ensure all rules use capture groups for the *exact* entity text to avoid including context words like 'Beschwerdesache'.",
  "strategy": "Refine regex patterns to be more precise about entity boundaries. Use negative lookahead or specific stopping characters (commas, addresses) to prevent capturing context words. Expand the allowed suffixes in the name pattern to include academic and professional degrees found in the training data (e.g., 'MA Bakk. art.', 'RgR PhD', 'Priv.-Doz.in'). Ensure titles are included in the match when they precede the name, but exclude the context trigger words (e.g., 'Beschwerdesache') from the final entity text.",
  "note": "Rules are updated to use specific capture groups ($1) for the entity text to ensure context words are excluded. Patterns are tightened to stop at commas or address indicators.",
  "rules": [
    {
      "name": "Party_Person",
      "description": "Extracts person names following context phrases like 'Beschwerdesache', 'gegen den', etc. Captures full name including suffixes, stopping at commas or addresses.",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "(?:Beschwerdesache|Beschwerde der|Beschwerde des|Antrag von|Vertreten durch|vertreten durch|des\\s+|der\\s+|gegen\\s+den|gegen\\s+die|gegen\\s+dem|mit\\s+dem|mit\\s+der)\\s+([A-Z][a-zäöüß]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+)*(?:\\s+(?:LL\\.M\\.|LL\\.B\\.|Bakk\\. iur\\.|Dipl\\.-Ing\\.|\u00d6kR|StB|RA|Steuermann|Wirtschaftspr\u00fcfer|KommR|Univ\\.-Prof\\.in|Hon\\.-Prof\\.in|Priv\\.-Doz\\.in|Mag\\.a|Dr\\.in|Dr\\.|KzlR|VetR|Mag\\.|Dr\\.|Prof\\.|Hon\\.-Prof\\.|Priv\\.-Doz\\.|Univ\\.-Prof\\.|Bakk\\. iur\\.|Dipl\\.-Ing\\.|\u00d6kR|LL\\.M\\.|LL\\.B\\.|StB|RA|KommR|RgR|PhD|Ing\\.)?)",
      "priority": 9,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities"
    },
    {
      "name": "Title_Person_General",
      "description": "Captures person names starting with a known title, including complex multi-part titles and suffixes. Ensures full name capture.",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "\\b((?:Dr\\.|Mag\\.|Prof\\.|Hon\\.-Prof\\.|Priv\\.-Doz\\.|Univ\\.-Prof\\.|Bakk\\. iur\\.|Dipl\\.-Ing\\.|\u00d6kR|LL\\.M\\.|LL\\.B\\.|StB|RA|KommR|KzlR|VetR|Mag\\.a|Dr\\.in|Dr\\.|Prof\\.in|Hon\\.-Prof\\.in|Priv\\.-Doz\\.in|Univ\\.-Prof\\.in|RgR|PhD|Ing\\.)\\s+)?([A-Z][a-zäöüß]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+)*(?:\\s+(?:LL\\.M\\.|LL\\.B\\.|Bakk\\. iur\\.|Dipl\\.-Ing\\.|\u00d6kR|StB|RA|Steuermann|Wirtschaftspr\u00fcfer|KommR|Univ\\.-Prof\\.in|Hon\\.-Prof\\.in|Priv\\.-Doz\\.in|Mag\\.a|Dr\\.in|Dr\\.|Prof\\.|Hon\\.-Prof\\.|Priv\\.-Doz\\.|Univ\\.-Prof\\.|Bakk\\. iur\\.|Dipl\\.-Ing\\.|\u00d6kR|LL\\.M\\.|LL\\.B\\.|StB|RA|KommR|RgR|PhD|Ing\\.)?)",
      "priority": 8,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1$2",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities"
    },
    {
      "name": "Name_After_Context",
      "description": "Captures person names appearing after context phrases like 'des', 'der', 'gegen den' without a preceding title, to catch names like 'Oswald Altmann'. Stops at commas.",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "(?:des\\s+|der\\s+|gegen\\s+den|gegen\\s+die|gegen\\s+dem|mit\\s+dem|mit\\s+der)\\s+([A-Z][a-zäöüß]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+)*(?:\\s+(?:LL\\.M\\.|LL\\.B\\.|Bakk\\. iur\\.|Dipl\\.-Ing\\.|\u00d6kR|StB|RA|Steuermann|Wirtschaftspr\u00fcfer|KommR|Univ\\.-Prof\\.in|Hon\\.-Prof\\.in|Priv\\.-Doz\\.in|Mag\\.a|Dr\\.in|Dr\\.|KzlR|VetR|Mag\\.|Dr\\.|Prof\\.|Hon\\.-Prof\\.|Priv\\.-Doz\\.|Univ\\.-Prof\\.|Bakk\\. iur\\.|Dipl\\.-Ing\\.|\u00d6kR|LL\\.M\\.|LL\\.B\\.|StB|RA|KommR|RgR|PhD|Ing\\.)?)",
      "priority": 7,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities"
    },
    {
      "name": "RgR_Person",
      "description": "Specific rule for 'RgR' (Rechtsgelehrter) titles which often appear with PhD or other suffixes and are missed by general title rules.",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "\\b(RgR\\s+(?:PhD\\s+)?[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+)*)",
      "priority": 6,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities"
    },
    {
      "name": "Ing_Person",
      "description": "Specific rule for 'Ing.' (Ingenieur) titles which are often missed.",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "\\b(Ing\\.[\\s]+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+)*)",
      "priority": 6,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities"
    }
  ],
  "deleted_rules": [
    "Party_Person",
    "Title_Person_General",
    "Name_After_Context"
  ]
}

{
  "analysis": "The current rules suffer from several critical issues: 1) The 'Title_Person_General' rule is too greedy, capturing trailing whitespace and failing on complex multi-title sequences (e.g., 'Mag.a Priv.-Doz.in Hon.-Prof.in'). 2) The 'Party_Person' and 'Context_Person' rules incorrectly include the context word 'Beschwerdesache' in the match, violating the requirement to extract only the name. 3) The 'Sentence_Start_Person' rule incorrectly matches 'Das Bundesfinanzgericht' because it starts with a capital letter and is followed by a verb. 4) The rules fail to capture names with suffixes like 'MA Bakk. art.' or 'RgR PhD' because the patterns are too rigid or stop too early.",
  "strategy": "1. Replace 'Title_Person_General' with a more robust pattern that handles multiple consecutive titles (e.g., 'Mag.a', 'Priv.-Doz.in') and stops correctly at whitespace or punctuation without capturing trailing spaces. 2. Fix 'Party_Person' to use a lookahead to exclude 'Beschwerdesache' from the capture group, ensuring only the name is extracted. 3. Fix 'Context_Person' to strictly exclude 'Beschwerdesache' and other non-name entities. 4. Replace 'Sentence_Start_Person' with a more specific pattern that excludes common organization names like 'Das Bundesfinanzgericht' and requires a name-like structure (e.g., Title + Name or two capitalized words followed by a specific context). 5. Add a specific rule for names with academic suffixes (e.g., 'MA Bakk. art.') to ensure they are captured as a single entity.",
  "rules": [
    {
      "name": "Title_Person_General",
      "description": "Captures person names starting with one or more known titles, handling complex title combinations and stopping at punctuation or whitespace.",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "\\b((?:Dr\\.|Mag\\.|Prof\\.|Hon\\.-Prof\\.|Priv\\.-Doz\\.|Univ\\.-Prof\\.|Bakk\\. iur\\.|Dipl\\.-Ing\\.|\u00d6kR|LL\\.M\\.|LL\\.B\\.|StB|RA|KommR|KzlR|VetR|Mag\\.a|Dr\\.in|Dr\\.|Prof\\.in|Hon\\.-Prof\\.in|Priv\\.-Doz\\.in|Univ\\.-Prof\\.in|HR|RgR|PhD|Ing\\.)(?:\\s+(?:Dr\\.|Mag\\.|Prof\\.|Hon\\.-Prof\\.|Priv\\.-Doz\\.|Univ\\.-Prof\\.|Bakk\\. iur\\.|Dipl\\.-Ing\\.|\u00d6kR|LL\\.M\\.|LL\\.B\\.|StB|RA|KommR|KzlR|VetR|Mag\\.a|Dr\\.in|Dr\\.|Prof\\.in|Hon\\.-Prof\\.in|Priv\\.-Doz\\.in|Univ\\.-Prof\\.in|HR|RgR|PhD|Ing\\.)?)*\\s+[A-Z][a-z\u00e4\u00f6\u00fc\u00df\u00dfe]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z\u00e4\u00f6\u00fc\u00df\u00dfe]+)*(?:\\s+(?:LL\\.M\\.|LL\\.B\\.|Bakk\\. iur\\.|Dipl\\.-Ing\\.|\u00d6kR|StB|RA|Steuermann|Wirtschaftspr\u00fcfer|KommR|Univ\\.-Prof\\.in|Hon\\.-Prof\\.in|Priv\\.-Doz\\.in|Mag\\.a|Dr\\.in|Dr\\.|Prof\\.|Hon\\.-Prof\\.|Priv\\.-Doz\\.|Univ\\.-Prof\\.|Bakk\\. iur\\.|Dipl\\.-Ing\\.|\u00d6kR|LL\\.M\\.|LL\\.B\\.|StB|RA|KommR|MA|Bakk\\. art\\.)?)",
      "priority": 8,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities"
    },
    {
      "name": "Party_Person",
      "description": "Extracts person names specifically after 'Beschwerdesache', ensuring the name is followed by an address or comma, and excluding the context word from the match.",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "Beschwerdesache\\s+([A-Z][a-z\u00e4\u00f6\u00fc\u00df\u00dfe]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z\u00e4\u00f6\u00fc\u00df\u00dfe]+)+)(?=\\s*,|\\s+[A-Z][a-z\u00e4\u00f6\u00fc\u00df\u00dfe]+\\s+\\d+|\\s+\\d+)",
      "priority": 9,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities"
    },
    {
      "name": "Context_Person",
      "description": "Captures person names appearing after context phrases like 'Frau', 'Herrn', 'des', 'der', 'gegen den', or at the start of a sentence, ensuring at least two words and excluding common false positives.",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "(?:^|\\s)(?:Frau\\s+|Herrn\\s+|des\\s+|der\\s+|gegen\\s+den|gegen\\s+die|gegen\\s+dem|mit\\s+dem|mit\\s+der)\\s+([A-Z][a-z\u00e4\u00f6\u00fc\u00df\u00dfe]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z\u00e4\u00f6\u00fc\u00df\u00dfe]+)+)(?![\\s,]*(?:Bescheid|Schreibens|Ordination|Patienten|Schriftf|Finanzamtes|Bescheide|Haftungsbescheide|Beschwerdef|S\u00e4umnisbeschwerde|Verfahren|Verwaltungs\u00fcbertretung|Einkommensteuer|Steuernummer|Zahl|Zl\\.|Jahr|Jahre|Abs\\.|lit\\.|Bf|Bf\\.)\\b)",
      "priority": 7,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities"
    },
    {
      "name": "Sentence_Start_Person",
      "description": "Captures person names appearing at the very start of a sentence or after a newline, ensuring at least two words and excluding organization names.",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "^(?:\\s*)([A-Z][a-z\u00e4\u00f6\u00fc\u00df\u00dfe]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z\u00e4\u00f6\u00fc\u00df\u00dfe]+)+)(?=\\s*,|\\s+der|\\s+des|\\s+ist|\\s+war|\\s+hat|\\s+hat\\s+durch|\\s+hat\\s+erkannt|\\s+hat\\s+bek\u00e4mpft)",
      "priority": 6,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities"
    },
    {
      "name": "Suffix_Person",
      "description": "Captures person names with specific academic or professional suffixes that might be missed by general title rules, ensuring the suffix is included.",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "\\b([A-Z][a-z\u00e4\u00f6\u00fc\u00df\u00dfe]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z\u00e4\u00f6\u00fc\u00df\u00dfe]+)+\\s+(?:MA|Bakk\\. art\\.|PhD|RgR|Ing\\.)(?:\\s+(?:MA|Bakk\\. art\\.|PhD|RgR|Ing\\.)?)?)",
      "priority": 5,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities"
    }
  ],
  "deleted_rules": []
}

  "analysis": "The current rules fail on several key patterns: 1) Names following '(Nachname wie Bf.)' are not captured. 2) Names following 'in der Beschwerdesache' are not captured. 3) The 'Title_Person_General' rule is too greedy, capturing trailing whitespace or splitting multi-part titles (e.g., 'Priv.-Doz. Univ.-Prof.' is split). 4) The 'Context_Person' rule has incorrect capture group usage ($name instead of $1) and fails to handle names without preceding context words (like 'Frau' or 'Herrn') when they appear after specific legal phrases. 5) False positives occur where titles are followed by non-person entities or where the regex captures too much (e.g., 'Dr. Nikolaus Wth GmbH-WP u Stbg').",
  "strategy": "1. Add a specific rule for names following '(Nachname wie Bf.)' to handle the most frequent failure case. 2. Add a specific rule for names following 'in der Beschwerdesache' (and similar variations like 'gegen den Bescheid des ... betreffend ...'). 3. Refine 'Title_Person_General' to be more precise with title sequences and stop strictly at whitespace/punctuation, removing the trailing space issue. 4. Fix 'Context_Person' to use correct capture groups and broaden the context to include 'des', 'der', 'gegen' without requiring 'Frau/Herrn' if the name is clearly a person (two capitalized words). 5.   "rules": [

      "description": "Captures person names starting with a known title, ensuring the full title and name are captured as a single entity, stopping strictly at punctuation or whitespace.",

      "content": "\\b((?:Dr\\.|Mag\\.|Prof\\.|Hon\\.-Prof\\.|Priv\\.-Doz\\.|Univ\\.-Prof\\.|Bakk\\. iur\\.|Dipl\\.-Ing\\.|\u00d6kR|LL\\.M\\.|LL\\.B\\.|StB|RA|KommR|KzlR|VetR|Mag\\.a|Dr\\.in|Dr\\.|Prof\\.in|Hon\\.-Prof\\.in|Priv\\.-Doz\\.in|Univ\\.-Prof\\.in|HR)\\s+[A-Z][a-z\u00e4\u00f6\u00fc\u00df\u00dfe]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z\u00e4\u00f6\u00fc\u00df\u00dfe]+)*(?:\\s+(?:LL\\.M\\.|LL\\.B\\.|Bakk\\. iur\\.|Dipl\\.-Ing\\.|\u00d6kR|StB|RA|Steuermann|Wirtschaftspr\u00fcfer|KommR|Univ\\.-Prof\\.in|Hon\\.-Prof\\.in|Priv\\.-Doz\\.in|Mag\\.a|Dr\\.in|Dr\\.|Prof\\.|Hon\\.-Prof\\.|Priv\\.-Doz\\.|Univ\\.-Prof\\.|Bakk\\. iur\\.|Dipl\\.-Ing\\.|\u00d6kR|LL\\.M\\.|LL\\.B\\.|StB|RA|KommR)?)*)",
      "priority": 8,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities"
    },
    {
      "name": "Nachname_Wie_Bf_Person",
      "description": "Captures person names appearing immediately after '(Nachname wie Bf.)' or similar parenthetical name placeholders.",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "\\(Nachname wie Bf\\.\\)\\s+([A-Z][a-z\u00e4\u00f6\u00fc\u00df\u00dfe]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z\u00e4\u00f6\u00fc\u00df\u00dfe]+)+)",
      "priority": 10,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities"
    },
    {
      "name": "Beschwerdesache_Person",
